mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces equity discount rates and increases present value of future corporate cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.98% on 2008-02-01 reflects ongoing easing from prior tightening cycle
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 5.0% signal deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and discretionary spending, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: When UNRATE > 5.0%, SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline below 1,100 thousand units signal weakening residential investment and construction demand, which cascades into reduced consumer wealth effects and lower aggregate demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,012 thousand units on 2008-01-01, below threshold
failure_condition: When HOUST < 1,100, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or correlation with forward returns becomes insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index declines signal weakening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, indicating reduced business investment and future earnings capacity. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward guidance deteriorates.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines > 0.5% produce positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales and housing starts jointly decline, signaling synchronized weakness in consumer spending and residential investment. This interaction indicates broad-based demand destruction that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The mechanism requires both series to weaken simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines > 1.0% month-over-month AND HOUST declines > 5.0% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 380,197 million on 2008-02-01, HOUST at 1,012 thousand on 2008-01-01
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST decline simultaneously, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment declines combined with rising unemployment rate signal accelerating labor market deterioration. This interaction indicates imminent earnings pressure as corporate cost structures adjust downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines > 0.2% month-over-month AND UNRATE rises > 0.2 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137,993 thousand on 2008-02-01, UNRATE at 4.8% on 2008-02-01
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and UNRATE move adversely, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In monetary tightening regimes (FEDFUNDS > 3.5%), inflation measured by CPI accelerates above 2.5% year-over-year, signaling real rate compression and policy constraint. This regime-conditional mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as rate hike expectations increase.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 2.98% on 2008-02-01, CPIAUCSL at 212.571 on 2008-02-01
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: In tightening regimes with elevated inflation, SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 62% of triggered instances, or regime-conditional coefficient reverses sign relative to easing regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth declines below 2.0% annualized rate, signaling late-cycle slowdown or early recession. This regime-conditional mechanism produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations compress and recession probability increases.
candidate_trigger: Annualized GDPC1 growth rate falls below 2.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,677.1 billion chained 2017 dollars on 2007-10-01
regime_hint: late-cycle slowdown or early recession regime
failure_condition: When annualized GDPC1 growth < 2.0%, SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
